framing: Computer science graduate and competitive gamer applying to game studio in Seoul

jd.title: Junior Gameplay Programmer
jd.style_note: Startup-technical: game studio posting, specific engine/language requirements, portfolio-focused

requirements.text: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Game Engineering, or equivalent
Strong C++ programming skills with understanding of memory management and multithreading
Experience with Unreal Engine 5 (Blueprints and C++ gameplay framework)
Understanding of game physics, collision detection, and character controller systems
Portfolio demonstrating shipped game projects or game jam entries
Experience with networked multiplayer gameplay programming (replication, lag compensation)
Familiarity with Perforce or similar version control for large binary repositories

responsibilities: Implement gameplay systems for Krafton's next multiplayer title using Unreal Engine 5
Develop character movement, weapon mechanics, and interaction systems in C++
Collaborate with designers to prototype and iterate on gameplay features
Optimise gameplay code for target frame rate (60fps) on PC and console platforms
Participate in code reviews and contribute to team coding standards

expected_output.summary: Recent BSc Computer Science graduate from KAIST (Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology) with game development focus. Created 3 game projects using Unity and C# during university, including a 2D roguelike that won Best Gameplay at Global Game Jam Seoul 2025. Competitive Valorant player (Immortal rank). 6-month internship at Nexon's Devcat studio working on Mabinogi content tools. Passionate about multiplayer gameplay systems.

experience.title: Game Development Intern
bullets: Developed internal content creation tools for Mabinogi franchise using C# and Unity Editor scripting
Implemented NPC dialogue branching system used by 4 content designers
Fixed 15+ gameplay bugs in live service title during internship period
Participated in weekly sprint reviews and daily standups with 12-person development team

experience.title: Project Lead — Student Game Projects
bullets: Led 4-person team developing '던전러너' (Dungeon Runner), a 2D roguelike in Unity (C#) — won Best Gameplay at Global Game Jam Seoul 2025
Built procedural dungeon generation system using BSP tree algorithm
Created enemy AI behaviour trees and combat system with hit detection and damage calculation
Published game on itch.io — 2,400+ downloads in first month

education: BSc Computer Science — KAIST (Korea Advanced Institute of Science and Technology) (2021-2025)

jd_requirements: Unreal Engine 5 Blueprints C++ gameplay framework
game physics collision detection character controller
deliberate_flaws: No Unreal Engine experience — all projects in Unity (JD requires UE5)
C++ is intermediate/coursework level only — JD wants 'strong C++ programming skills'
No game physics or character controller experience (built 2D roguelike, not 3D character movement)
No multiplayer/networked gameplay experience (all projects are single-player)
No Perforce experience (used Git and Plastic SCM)
No performance optimisation experience (60fps target, frame budgeting)
No collision detection system implementation (used Unity's built-in physics)
All experience is Unity/C# — would need significant ramp-up for UE5/C++ codebase
Internship was on content tools, not gameplay programming
